content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CounalssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (9t6) 322-0886
January 27,2005
Pamela A. McCarthy, City Clerk
City of Bakersfield
Office of the City Clerk
1501 Truxtun Avenue
Bakersfield, CA 93301
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-05-002
Dear Ms. McCarthy:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r The city clerk for the City of
Bakersfield is the filing officer for certain campaign reports required to be filed under the
Act. The Act provides that the filing officer has a duty to determine whether the required
documents conform on their face with the requirements of the Act.2
QUESTION
Can late contribution reports be filed with the city clerk's office by e-mail as long
as the e-mail account is reviewed daily and the account is a shared account that can be
accessed by more than one person?
CONCLUSION
No. The Act does not provide for the filing of late contribution reports by e-mail
' Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
1 8 1 09- I 8997, of the California Code of Regulations. All statutory references herein are to the Government
Code unless otherwise indicated. All regulatoryreferences herein are to Title 2 of the Califomia Code of
Regulations unless otherwise indicated.
2 Section 81010.


File No. 4-05-002
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are the city clerk for the City of Bakersfield. The Act requires candidates
and committees to file late contribution reports in such offices as designated.3 As the
county clerk for the City of Bakersfield, your office is a filing location for certain late
contribution reports.
ANALYSIS
Section 84203, subdivision (b)a provides that:
"(b) A late contribution shall be reported by facsimile transmission,
telegram, guaranteed overnight mail through the United States postal
Service, or personal delivery within 24 hours of the time it is made in the
case of the candidate or committee that makes the contribution and within
24 hours of the time it is received in the case of the recipient."
The Act makes no provision for filing late contribution reports by e-mail.
Accordingly, e-mail filings do not comply with the reporting provisions of the Act.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By: Wil
Division
wJLjg
I:\Adviceltrs A-\05-002
3 Sections 84203 and84215. Section 84215 provides that two copies ofcampaign reports and
statements be filed "with the clerk of the county with the largest number of registered voters in the districts
affected." (Section 84275, subdivision (bX2), (c) and (d).)
a Please note that "telegram" is no longer an acceptable method for delivery.
